title: "Missed a time slot?" simulation error when verilated with --timing and --x-initial-edge
body:
The following code causes simulation to abort when verilated with `--timing` and `--x-initial-edge`:
```SystemVerilog
module test; 
initial begin 
    #10; 
end 
endmodule
```

The error:
```
%Error: /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp:83: %Error: Encountered process that should've been resumed at an earlier simulation time. Missed a time slot?
```
Reproducing (`--binary` implies `--timing`):
```
echo "module test; initial begin #10; end endmodule" > test.sv
verilator --binary --x-initial-edge test.sv
./obj_dir/Vtest
```
verilator --version:
```
Verilator 5.040 2025-08-30 rev v5.040 & Verilator 5.041 devel rev v5.040-358-gb65200923
```
OS: Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS (and the one used in verilator docker container)

Seems to be related to #5723, specifically this [comment](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/5723#issuecomment-3407078256) there.
code_blocks:
module test; 
initial begin 
    #10; 
end 
endmodule
%Error: /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp:83: %Error: Encountered process that should've been resumed at an earlier simulation time. Missed a time slot?
echo "module test; initial begin #10; end endmodule" > test.sv
verilator --binary --x-initial-edge test.sv
./obj_dir/Vtest
Verilator 5.040 2025-08-30 rev v5.040 & Verilator 5.041 devel rev v5.040-358-gb65200923
